assertion_results.text: Output uses NDKNip46Signer (or equivalent NDK NIP-46 signer class) for the connection
assertion_results.evidence: import NDK, { NDKNip46Signer } from "@nostr-dev-kit/ndk"; — then: const signer = NDKNip46Signer.nostrconnect(ndk, nip46Relay, savedLocalNsec, { name: "My Nostr App", ... });

assertion_results.text: Output generates a nostrconnect:// URI containing the client pubkey, relays, a required secret, and app name
assertion_results.evidence: const connectUri = signer.nostrConnectUri; — URI example shown: nostrconnect://83f3b2ae...?relay=wss%3A%2F%2Frelay.primal.net&perms=...&name=My+Nostr+App&secret=a1b2c3d4

assertion_results.text: Output includes requested permissions in the nostrconnect URI (perms parameter with method:kind format)
assertion_results.evidence: perms: ["sign_event:1", "sign_event:30023", "nip44_encrypt", "nip44_decrypt"] — shown in NDKNip46Signer.nostrconnect() call and in the URI example: &perms=sign_event%3A1%2Csign_event%3A30023%2Cnip44_encrypt%2Cnip44_decrypt

assertion_results.text: Output shows waiting for the signer to connect before using the signer (blockUntilReady or equivalent)
assertion_results.evidence: const user = await signer.blockUntilReady(); — with comment: 'This blocks until the remote signer scans the QR and responds'

assertion_results.text: Output explains that the client MUST validate the secret returned in the connect response to prevent spoofing
assertion_results.evidence: Security Considerations section: 'Always validate the secret: When using nostrconnect://, include a secret parameter and validate that the connect response returns the same secret. This prevents connection spoofing.'

assertion_results.text: Output explains switch_relays: the signer controls which relays are used, client must call switch_relays after connecting and update local relay state accordingly
assertion_results.evidence: Dedicated section 'How switch_relays Works and Why It Matters' explains: 'allows the remote signer to tell the client to change which relays are used', 'Relay Sovereignty: The remote signer should always control which relays are used', 'Immediately update its local relay state when it receives a non-null response', 'Send all subsequent NIP-46 requests on the new relays', 'call switch_relays right after the connect handshake completes'

assertion_results.text: Output uses kind 24133 (or references it) as the NIP-46 event kind
assertion_results.evidence: NIP-46 Protocol Details section: 'All NIP-46 communication uses kind 24133 events. Both requests and responses use this same kind.' Also referenced throughout: 'kind:24133 events', 'kind 24133'

assertion_results.text: Output mentions that all kind:24133 content is encrypted with NIP-44 (not NIP-04)
assertion_results.evidence: Encryption section: 'All message content is encrypted using NIP-44 encryption. The client encrypts to the remote-signer-pubkey, and the signer encrypts to the client-pubkey.'
